title: Sprouts Bhel

subs.name: canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed
subs.notes: no sprouting required; adds protein and substance

subs.name: a few drops of tomato ketchup mixed with lemon juice
subs.notes: approximate sweet-sour balance when ready-made chutney is not available

steps.text: Steam or microwave the sprouted moong for 2–3 minutes until barely cooked but still crunchy — raw sprouts can cause digestive discomfort. Alternatively, blanch in boiling water 1 minute and drain. Cool completely.
In a large bowl combine onion, tomato, cucumber, green chilli, and coriander leaves. Add lemon juice, chaat masala, and salt and mix well.
Add the cooled sprouts and roasted peanuts and toss to coat in the seasoning. Taste and adjust salt and lemon.
Add the puffed rice just before serving and toss quickly — add it early and it turns soggy within minutes. Drizzle tamarind chutney over if using.
Serve immediately in bowls or on plates. The bhel must be eaten within 5 minutes of adding the puffed rice to keep the crunch.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: sprouts → canned chickpeas, drained and rinsed (no sprouting required; adds protein and substance); puffed-rice → crushed papad or murmura mix (same crunch, different flavour); tamarind → a few drops of tomato ketchup mixed with lemon juice (approximate sweet-sour balance when ready-made chutney is not available)
